PCI_DSS_v4.details: Fails key requirements including TLS and auth

LGPD.details: Data exposure via error responses and SQLi

findings.title: SQL Injection in Search Endpoint
findings.description: The product search endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection via the 'q' query parameter. An unauthenticated attacker can extract the entire database including user credentials, payment information, and internal configuration.
findings.evidence:
Payload: 1' UNION SELECT email,password,NULL FROM users--
Response returned user emails and password hashes.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

findings.title: Broken Authentication - JWT Token Forgery
findings.description: JWT tokens are signed with a hardcoded secret key 'secureshop2024' found in source code. An attacker can forge valid tokens for any user including administrators.
findings.evidence: JWT secret discovered as 'secureshop2024'. Forged admin token accepted for 365 days.
findings.remediation: Rotate JWT secret immediately. Use JWKs for key rotation. Store secrets in vault.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in Error Responses
findings.description: Detailed error responses expose internal stack traces, database connection strings with credentials, Stripe live API keys, AWS secrets, and internal IP addresses.
findings.evidence: Error response contained: database URL with password, stripe_key sk_live_51N...xk8v, AWS secret key, internal IP 10.0.3.42.
findings.remediation: Disable verbose errors in production. Return generic error messages. Log details server-side only.

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via Image Upload
findings.description: The product image upload feature accepts URLs without validation, allowing SSRF attacks against internal services and cloud metadata endpoints (AWS IMDSv2).
findings.evidence: Successfully retrieved AWS IAM credentials from http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/
findings.remediation: Implement URL allowlisting. Block RFC 1918 and cloud metadata IP ranges. Validate image type after download.

findings.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference on Order API
findings.description: Any authenticated user can view order details for any order ID. No ownership verification is performed when accessing /api/v1/orders/{id}.
findings.evidence: User A's token successfully retrieved order 10247 belonging to user B, including email, shipping address, and credit card last 4 digits.
findings.remediation: Implement ownership checks: verify the authenticated user ID matches the order's user ID.

findings.title: Reflected XSS in Search Results
findings.description: User input from the search query parameter is reflected in the HTML response without encoding, enabling reflected XSS attacks.
findings.evidence: Payload <script>alert(1)</script> rendered unescaped in search results page title.
findings.remediation: Implement HTML entity output encoding. Deploy Content-Security-Policy header.

findings.title: Weak Session Management - No Expiration
findings.description: Session tokens have no expiration (expires_at: null). Cookies lack HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite flags. Stolen tokens remain valid indefinitely.
findings.evidence: Login response: expires_at:null. Cookie flags: Path=/ only (no HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite).
findings.remediation: Set session expiration (24h idle, 30d absolute). Add HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict flags.

findings.title: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 Still Enabled
findings.description: The server accepts connections using deprecated TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 protocols, vulnerable to POODLE and BEAST attacks. PCI-DSS 4.0 requires TLS 1.2 minimum.
findings.evidence: Successfully connected using TLSv1 with cipher AES256-SHA. TLS 1.1 also accepted.
findings.remediation: Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1. Enforce TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites only.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on API
findings.description: No rate limiting is enforced on any API endpoint. 1000 login attempts completed without throttle or 429 response. No RateLimit-* headers present.
findings.evidence: 1000 POST /api/v1/auth/login requests returned 401 (not 429). No rate limit headers in any response.
findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting: 100 req/min per IP, 5 login attempts/min. Return HTTP 429.

findings.title: Open Redirect on Logout Endpoint
findings.description: The logout endpoint redirects to arbitrary external URLs specified in the 'next' query parameter, enabling phishing attacks.
findings.evidence: Redirect to https://evil.com/phishing via HTTP 302 Location header.
findings.remediation: Validate redirect URLs against allowlist. Reject non-whitelisted domains.

findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy header is set, providing no defense-in-depth against XSS attacks or data exfiltration via JavaScript.
findings.evidence: curl -sI target | grep CSP returned no results.
findings.remediation: Add: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

findings.title: Clickjacking - Missing X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors is configured, allowing the site to be embedded in iframes for clickjacking attacks.
findings.evidence: Page successfully loaded in an iframe from an external domain.
findings.remediation: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'

findings.title: Directory Listing Enabled on /uploads/
findings.description: Directory listing is enabled on /uploads/ exposing invoices, receipts, customer documents, and a SQL database backup.
findings.evidence: Directory listing revealed: invoices/, receipts_2024/, customer_documents/, database_backup_jan.sql
findings.remediation: Disable directory listing (autoindex off). Block access to sensitive directories.

findings.title: CORS Wildcard with Credentials
findings.description: CORS is configured with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * along with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, allowing any domain to make authenticated requests.
findings.evidence: Request with Origin: https://evil.com received Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Allow-Credentials: true.
findings.remediation: Replace wildcard with specific allowed origins. Never use * with credentials.

findings.title: Stored XSS via Product Review Forms
findings.description: Product review text is stored and rendered without sanitization, allowing persistent XSS that fires for every visitor to the product page.
findings.evidence: Submitted review with rendered and executed on next page load.
findings.remediation: Sanitize user input on both input and output. Use a library like DOMPurify.

findings.title: Verbose Server Headers Reveal Technology Stack
findings.description: HTTP headers reveal nginx 1.18.0 (Ubuntu), Express.js, ASP.NET version, aiding attackers in targeting known vulnerabilities.
findings.evidence:
Server: nginx/1.18.0 (Ubuntu)
X-Powered-By: Express
X-AspNet-Version: 4.0.30319
findings.remediation: Remove/hide Server header (server_tokens off). Remove X-Powered-By header.

findings.title: CSRF Token Missing on Form Submissions
findings.description: State-changing API endpoints do not require CSRF tokens. Attackers can trigger actions like address changes from victim's browser using stored credentials.
findings.evidence: Successfully changed address using only session cookie, no CSRF token required.
findings.remediation: Implement CSRF token generation and validation on all state-changing endpoints.

findings.title: Password Hashing Using MD5
findings.description: User passwords are hashed with MD5, a broken algorithm that can be cracked at billions of attempts per second using GPU-based tools.
findings.evidence: Password hash e10adc3949ba59abbe56e057f20f883e (MD5 of '123456') recovered in under 1 second.
findings.remediation: Migrate to bcrypt (cost factor >= 12) or Argon2id. Force password reset for all users.

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header allows browsers to MIME-sniff responses, potentially enabling drive-by download attacks.
findings.evidence: No X-Content-Type-Options header detected in any HTTP response.
findings.remediation: Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff to all HTTP responses.

findings.title: HTTP TRACE Method Enabled
findings.description: The HTTP TRACE method is enabled, potentially enabling Cross-Site Tracing (XST) attacks to steal credentials.
findings.evidence: OPTIONS response includes TRACE in the Allow header.
findings.remediation: Disable TRACE method in web server configuration.

findings.title: robots.txt Reveals Sensitive Paths
findings.description: The robots.txt file reveals the existence of admin panels, internal APIs, config directories, and backup locations.
findings.evidence: robots.txt disallows: /admin/, /api/v1/internal/, /api/v1/admin/, /.env, /config/, /backups/, /phpmyadmin/
findings.remediation: Use minimal robots.txt. Do not reveal sensitive paths. Use proper access controls instead.

findings.title: Suspicious Log Patterns - Potential Active Exploitation
findings.description: Log analysis over 30 days reveals brute force attacks (2,847 login attempts from single IP), SQL injection probing, and unusual data exfiltration patterns (47.3 GB downloaded vs 2.1 GB baseline).
findings.evidence: 23 unique IPs from Tor exit nodes. 47.3 GB data downloaded by 194.26.29.x over 3 days. 2,847 brute force attempts from 45.155.205.x.
findings.remediation: Implement IP-based blocking, WAF rules, and automated alerting for anomalous patterns.

findings.title: Outdated Dependencies with Known CVEs
findings.description: 14 vulnerable npm packages identified, including lodash (CVE-2020-8203), express (CVE-2022-24999), jsonwebtoken (CVE-2022-23529), and missing security middleware (helmet).
findings.evidence: lodash@4.17.15, express@4.17.1, axios@0.21.1, jsonwebtoken@8.5.1. helmet not installed.
findings.remediation: Run npm audit fix. Update all dependencies to latest versions. Install helmet middleware.
